event.summary: Recorded passing validation; acceptance remains a separate explicit transition.

acceptance.proof: A public XmlLeafElement infallibly moves exactly one accepted XmlElementName and one accepted XmlCharacterData into private state. Borrowed access and consuming recovery preserve both component limits, exact contents, and owned string allocations without cloning, normalization, revalidation, or re-encoding.

Construction accepts only the existing LSLC-001B name and LSLC-001C character-data types and introduces no raw-string entrypoint, limit, error, allocation, escaping, namespace, or interpretation policy. Focused positive and damaged tests cover empty character data, legal Unicode, colon as syntax only, and already represented predefined-reference text.

The accepted value owns exactly the two validated components and exposes no child, parent, attribute, declaration, comment, processing-instruction, mixed-content, root, tag-spelling, raw-byte, serializer, parser, decoder, entity, metadata-tree, or stream-info mapping surface.

A separate LSLC-001D local-results overlay binds independently authored tests only to the accepted LSLC-001B and LSLC-001C component contracts. Every LSLC-001A oracle and candidate role remains not-observed with null evidence, and LSLC-001C greater-than escaping remains candidate-owned local policy.

The crate remains dependency-, feature-, unsafe-, I/O-, activation-, parser-, serialization-, protocol-, wire-, runtime-, FFI-, adapter-, provider-, and device-free; historical evidence remains unchanged and the feature/effect lock remains empty and inert.

document.commit_policy: Commit the accepted leaf-element composition separately from tag spelling, element trees, document assembly, stream-info mapping, oracle capture, compatibility comparison, protocol, transport, adapters, providers, or runtime work.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the leaf-only composition contract and keep tag, tree, document, LSL mapping, oracle, protocol, and runtime authority closed.
Describe the exact two-component local composition without presenting it as XML tag syntax, serialization, document structure, or liblsl compatibility.
Review whether composition of accepted values adds reusable authority guidance; retain the shared skill unchanged if its existing proposal, value, and representation rules are sufficient.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review the clean-source and public-evidence boundary; retain the shared router unchanged if existing provenance guidance covers the unit.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

non_scope: Start-tag, end-tag, or empty-element spelling; child elements; mixed content; parent indices; element trees; root cardinality; XML declarations, attributes, namespaces, comments, processing instructions, CDATA sections, schemas, queries, or complete documents.

Raw-string entrypoints, new XML validation, new escaping or entity policy, cloning or reallocation of accepted component strings, parsing, decoding, serialization, byte output, canonicalization, or endpoint representation.

Mapping MetadataTree, StreamDescriptor, or StreamDefinition into LSL stream-info roles; core-field order, omission rules, numeric or channel-format spelling, desc/channel metadata, runtime/misc fields, or exact liblsl bytes.

Oracle capture, candidate differential comparison, official-liblsl behavior claims, protocol versions, wire framing, discovery, resolution, networking, sockets, inlets, outlets, transport, buffering, queues, threads, clocks, recovery, providers, FFI, native loading, adapters, or language bindings.

Cargo dependencies, Cargo features, unsafe Rust, additional crates, generated protocol material, copied or translated liblsl or rLSL source, production liblsl integration, device validation, or Morphospace runtime authority.
document.objective: Implement a dependency-free XmlLeafElement composition value that infallibly owns one accepted XmlElementName and one accepted XmlCharacterData, preserves their exact bounded state and allocations, and opens no tag spelling, element-tree, document, stream-info mapping, parser, serializer, protocol, wire, or runtime behavior.
outputs: A dependency-free XmlLeafElement API with private two-component state, infallible move construction, borrowed access, and allocation-preserving consuming recovery.
Focused positive and damaged tests proving exact component preservation and the absence of duplicated validation, allocation, representation, namespace, tag, tree, document, and LSL-mapping policy.
A separate LSLC-001D local-results overlay, deterministic focused checker, aggregate-gate routing, and lifecycle-neutral public documentation.
